name: RhinoMath
summary: Provides constants and static methods that are additional to Math.

methods.name: Clamp(Double, Double, Double)
methods.signature: public static double Clamp(double value, double bound1, double bound2)
methods.summary: Limits a Double to be specified within an interval of two numbers, by specifying a fixed minimum and maximum.

params.name: bound2
params.doc: A second bound. This does not necessarily need to be larger or smaller than bound1.

methods.name: Clamp(Int32, Int32, Int32)
methods.signature: public static int Clamp(int value, int bound1, int bound2)
methods.summary: Restricts a Int32 to be specified within an interval of two integers.

params.name: bound2
params.doc: A second bound. This does not necessarily need to be larger or smaller than bound1.

methods.name: CRC32(UInt32, Byte[])
methods.signature: public static uint CRC32(uint currentRemainder, byte[] buffer)
methods.summary: Advances the cyclic redundancy check value remainder given a byte array. http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cyclic_redundancy_check.

params.name: currentRemainder
params.doc: The remainder from which to start.

params.name: buffer
params.doc: The value to add to the current remainder.

methods.name: CRC32(UInt32, Double)
methods.signature: public static uint CRC32(uint currentRemainder, double value)
methods.summary: Advances the cyclic redundancy check value remainder given a Double. http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cyclic_redundancy_check.

params.name: currentRemainder
params.doc: The remainder from which to start.

params.name: value
params.doc: The value to add to the current remainder.

methods.name: CRC32(UInt32, Int32)
methods.signature: public static uint CRC32(uint currentRemainder, int value)
methods.summary: Advances the cyclic redundancy check value remainder given a Int32. http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cyclic_redundancy_check.

params.name: currentRemainder
params.doc: The remainder from which to start.

params.name: value
params.doc: The value to add to the current remainder.

methods.name: EpsilonEquals(Double, Double, Double)
methods.signature: public static bool EpsilonEquals(double x, double y, double epsilon)
methods.summary: Compare two doubles for equality within some "epsilon" range

methods.name: EpsilonEquals(Single, Single, Single)
methods.signature: public static bool EpsilonEquals(float x, float y, float epsilon)
methods.summary: Compare to floats for equality within some "epsilon" range

methods.name: EvaluateNormal
methods.signature: public static bool EvaluateNormal(int limitDirection, Vector3d ds, Vector3d dt, Vector3d dss, Vector3d dst, Vector3d dtt, out Vector3d n)
methods.summary: Expert tool to evaluate surface unit normal.

params.name: limitDirection
params.doc: Determines which direction is used to compute the limit, where: 0 = default, 1 = from quadrant I, 2 = from quadrant II, etc.

methods.name: EvaluateNormalPartials
methods.signature: public static bool EvaluateNormalPartials(Vector3d ds, Vector3d dt, Vector3d dss, Vector3d dst, Vector3d dtt, out Vector3d ns, out Vector3d nt)
methods.summary: Expert tool to evaluate partial derivatives of surface unit normal.

params.name: ns
params.doc: First partial derivative of unit normal. If the Jacobian is degenerate, ns is set to zero.

params.name: nt
params.doc: First partial derivative of unit normal. If the Jacobian is degenerate, nt is set to zero.

returns.doc: true if Jacobian is non-degenerate, false if Jacobian is degenerate.

methods.name: EvaluateSectionalCurvature
methods.signature: public static bool EvaluateSectionalCurvature(Vector3d ds, Vector3d dt, Vector3d dss, Vector3d dst, Vector3d dtt, Vector3d planeNormal, out Vector3d k)
methods.summary: Expert tool to evaluate sectional curvature from surface derivatives and section plane normal.

params.name: k
params.doc: Sectional curvature. Curvature of the intersection curve of the surface and plane through the surface point where the partial derivatives were evaluated.

returns.doc: True if successful. False if first partials are not linearly independent, in which case the k is set to zero.

methods.name: IntIndexToString
methods.signature: public static string IntIndexToString(int index)
methods.summary: Portrays an Int32 index in text.

returns.doc: The text representation of the int index.

methods.name: IsValidDouble
methods.signature: public static bool IsValidDouble(double x)
methods.summary: Determines whether a Double value is valid within the RhinoCommon context. Rhino does not use Double.NaN by convention, so this test evaluates to true if:x is not equal to RhinoMath.UnsetValueSystem.Double.IsNaN(x) evaluates to falseSystem.Double.IsInfinity(x) evaluates to false

params.name: x
params.doc: Double number to test for validity.

returns.doc: true if the number if valid, false if the number is NaN, Infinity or Unset.

methods.name: IsValidSingle
methods.signature: public static bool IsValidSingle(float x)
methods.summary: Determines whether a Single value is valid within the RhinoCommon context. Rhino does not use Single.NaN by convention, so this test evaluates to true if:x is not equal to RhinoMath.UnsetValue,System.Single.IsNaN(x) evaluates to falseSystem.Single.IsInfinity(x) evaluates to false

params.name: x
params.doc: Single number to test for validity.

returns.doc: true if the number if valid, false if the number is NaN, Infinity or Unset.

methods.name: MetersPerUnit
methods.signature: public static double MetersPerUnit(UnitSystem units)
methods.summary: Return number of meters per one unit of a given unit system

methods.name: ParseNumber
methods.signature: public static double ParseNumber(string expression)
methods.summary: Evaluates command line math expression.

methods.name: ToDegrees
methods.signature: public static double ToDegrees(double radians)
methods.summary: Convert an angle from radians to degrees.

params.name: radians
params.doc: Radians to convert (180 degrees equals pi radians).

methods.name: ToRadians
methods.signature: public static double ToRadians(double degrees)
methods.summary: Convert an angle from degrees to radians.

params.name: degrees
params.doc: Degrees to convert (180 degrees equals pi radians).

methods.name: TryParseNumber
methods.signature: public static bool TryParseNumber(string expression, out double result)
methods.summary: Evaluates command line math expression.

methods.name: UnitScale(UnitSystem, UnitSystem)
methods.signature: public static double UnitScale(UnitSystem from, UnitSystem to)
methods.summary: Computes the scale factor for changing the measurements unit systems.

params.name: to
params.doc: The system to convert measurements into.

methods.name: UnitScale(UnitSystem, Double, UnitSystem, Double)
methods.signature: public static double UnitScale(UnitSystem from, double fromMetersPerUnit, UnitSystem to, double toMetersPerUnit)
methods.summary: Computes the scale factor for changing the measurements unit systems.

params.name: fromMetersPerUnit
params.doc: For custom units, specify the meters per unit.

params.name: to
params.doc: The system to convert measurements into.

params.name: toMetersPerUnit
params.doc: For custom units, specify the meters per unit.

methods.name: Wrap
methods.signature: public static double Wrap(double value, double bound1, double bound2)
methods.summary: Limits a Double to be specified within an interval of two numbers by repeating the available interval cyclically.

params.name: bound2
params.doc: A second bound. This does not necessarily need to be larger or smaller than bound1.

properties.name: DefaultAngleTolerance
properties.summary: Represents the default angle tolerance, used when no other values are provided. This is one degree, expressed in radians.
properties.remarks: This is equivalent to openNURBS ON_DEFAULT_ANGLE_TOLERANCE.

properties.name: DefaultDistanceToleranceMillimeters
properties.summary: Get Rhino's default distance tolerance in millimeters.
properties.remarks: This is equivalent to openNURBS ON_DEFAULT_DISTANCE_TOLERANCE_MM.

properties.name: Epsilon
properties.summary: Gets the value of DBL_EPSILON, which is the smallest positive floating point number x such that 1 + x != 1. This is different than Double.Epsilon which is the smallest positive Double value that is greater than zero.
properties.remarks: This is equivalent to openNURBS ON_EPSILON.

properties.name: QuarterPI
properties.summary: One eigth of a rotation. 45 degrees. 0.78...

properties.name: SqrtEpsilon
properties.summary: Represents a default value that is used when comparing square roots. This value is several orders of magnitude larger than ZeroTolerance.
properties.remarks: This is equivalent to openNURBS ON_SQRT_EPSILON.

properties.name: Tau
properties.summary: Ratio of circumference divided by radius. Full rotation. 360 degrees. 6.28...

properties.name: UnsetIntIndex
properties.summary: When signed int values are used in a context where 0 and small negative values are valid indices and there needs to be a value that indicates the index is not set.
properties.remarks: This is equivalent to openNURBS ON_UNSET_INT_INDEX.

properties.name: UnsetSingle
properties.summary: Gets the single precision floating point number that is considered 'unset' in Rhino.
properties.remarks: This is equivalent to openNURBS ON_UNSET_FLOAT.

properties.name: UnsetValue
properties.summary: Gets the Rhino standard Unset value. Use this value rather than Double.NaN when a bogus floating point value is required.
properties.remarks: This is equivalent to openNURBS ON_UNSET_VALUE.

properties.name: ZeroTolerance
properties.summary: Gets Rhino's Zero Tolerance constant, which is 2^-32. In cases when an absolute "zero" tolerance is required to compare model space coordinates, ZeroTolerance is used. The value of ZeroTolerance should be no smaller than Epsilon and should be several orders of magnitude smaller than SqrtEpsilon.
properties.remarks: This is equivalent to openNURBS ON_ZERO_TOLERANCE.
